selectedAdapter.redistributionPolicy: Allowed with MIT license notice bundled next to packaged adapter artifacts.

dap.boundary: Electron2D.Editor -> Electron2D.ManagedDebugging -> DAP stdio -> netcoredbg -> Electron2D game process

selectedAdapter.updatePolicy: Pin a release tag, refresh platform assets and source-build outputs through the T-0163 validation checklist before updating the bundled adapter.

t0160Handoff.adapterHostContract: Start netcoredbg as a child process with --interpreter=vscode, speak Debug Adapter Protocol over stdio, and map Editor breakpoints/source anchors to DAP requests.

candidateReview.reason: Mature open-source .NET debugger with VS Code Debug Adapter Protocol mode, launch and attach support, Windows/Linux binary release assets, source-build path for macOS arm64, and compatible redistribution terms.

Not selected for Electron2D distribution because the Microsoft debugger adapter licensing and distribution model is tied to Microsoft IDE workflows and is not a permissive redistributable dependency for this engine.

Promising pure .NET DAP adapter, but no GitHub release/package assets were available during validation; using it would make T-0160 depend on a young source-only debugger before its packaging story is stable.

platformTargets.requiredReleaseGate: Run the same DAP launch and attach smoke on a Linux x64 host before shipping Linux editor/debugger packages.

sourceBuild.requiredHost: macOS arm64 with .NET SDK, CMake, Homebrew and clang toolchain
sourceBuild.reason: The upstream 3.1.3-1062 release publishes osx-amd64 but not osx-arm64. NetCoreDbg source is MIT licensed and documents a macOS arm64 build path, so Electron2D must produce and smoke-test its own osx-arm64 artifact in the release packaging pipeline.

platformTargets.requiredReleaseGate: Build osx-arm64 from the pinned source tag, bundle MIT license notice, then run DAP launch and attach smoke on macOS arm64 before shipping macOS editor/debugger packages.

limitations: setBreakpoints may initially report a pending breakpoint; Electron2D must treat the later stopped:breakpoint event and resolved frame as the binding confirmation.

NetCoreDbg initialize response does not advertise a native DAP restart request; Electron2D 0.1.0 restart must be implemented by the Editor as disconnect plus a new launch over the same adapter package.
macOS arm64 is a source-build packaging target for Electron2D because the selected upstream release does not publish osx-arm64 binaries.
Remote Android, iOS and WebAssembly debugging remain outside Electron2D 0.1.0.
